key_insight: CORAL uses reinforcement learning to generate amyloid-ligand docking poses that explicitly reward cooperative stacking along fibril grooves, matching a binding mode that standard docking tools miss.
engineering_relevance: This enables more realistic structure-based screening for amyloid-targeted diagnostics and therapeutics, where accurate pose prediction can improve hit identification and affinity ranking.
method: Generative docking model trained with reinforcement learning
Reward combines protein-ligand affinity and ligand-ligand cooperative stacking energy
Designed for cross-b2 groove geometry of amyloid fibrils
evidence_signal: The authors report improved pose quality and better affinity correlation on experimentally resolved structures plus a curated amyloid-ligand evaluation set vetted by domain experts.
watch_item: The curated evaluation set is partly model-generated and expert-validated, so broader generalization to diverse amyloid targets and truly unseen chemistries still needs further validation.
paper_title: CORAL: Learning Amyloid Fibril Ligand Docking with Cooperative Binding Rewards
